questions.question: Define harmonic function. Show that u = 3x²y + x² − y³ − y² is a harmonic function. Find the analytic function for which the given function is a real part.
State Cauchy integral formula for derivative. Evaluate ∮_c z⁶/(2z−1)⁶ dz, where c is the unit circle |z| = 1, counterclockwise.
Show that the transformation w = (2z+3)/(z−4) maps the circle x² + y² − 4x = 0 onto the straight line 4u + 3 = 0 in the w-plane.
Find the Taylor's and Laurent's series of the function f(z) = (z²−1)/[(z+2)(z+3)].

or_question.question: State Cauchy Residue Theorem. By applying Cauchy Residue Theorem, evaluate ∮_c (4−3z)/[z(z−1)(z−2)] dz where C: |z| = 3/2.

questions.question: State and prove initial value theorem and find the inverse z-transform of F(z) = (z²−3z)/[(z−5)(z+2)].
Solve the difference equation by using Z-transform: y_{n+2} − 4y_{n+1} + 4y_n = 2ⁿ, where y₀ = 0, y₁ = 1.
Show that ∫₀^∞ (sin πw · sin xw)/(1−w²) dw = (π/2) sin x if 0 ≤ x ≤ π, and 0 if x > π.
Find Fourier cosine transform of f(x) = e^{−mx} for m > 0. Then prove that ∫₀^∞ (cos kx)/(1+x²) dx = (π/2)e^{−k}.
Find u(x, t) from the one-dimensional wave equation ∂²u/∂t² = c² ∂²u/∂x² with boundary conditions u(0,t) = 0 = u(L,t), initial deflection f(x), and initial velocity ∂u/∂t|_{t=0} = g(x).
questions.eq: \frac{\partial^2 u}{\partial t^2} = c^2\frac{\partial^2 u}{\partial x^2},\quad u(0,t)=0=u(L,t),\quad \left.\frac{\partial u}{\partial t}\right|_{t=0}=g(x)

questions.question: Define partial differential equation. By separating variables, solve: (i) u_{xy} − u = 0   (ii) x·u_{xy} + 2y·u = 0.
Find the temperature in a laterally insulated bar of length L = 20 cm whose ends are kept at zero temperature, assuming that the initial temperature is f(x) = x if 0 < x < L/2, (L−x) if L/2 < x < L.
questions.eq: f(x) = \begin{cases} x & 0 < x < L/2 \\ L-x & L/2 < x < L \end{cases},\quad L=20\text{ cm}

questions.question: Express the Laplacian ∇²u = ∂²u/∂x² + ∂²u/∂y² in polar coordinates.

options.question: Show that f(z) = zz̄ is not an analytic function.
Find z-transform of z(aⁿ).
Solve the partial differential equation u_{xx} + 9u = 0.
Write equation of an ellipsoid. Sketch it with centre and axis of symmetry.

questions.question: Prove that u = y³ − 3x²y is a harmonic function. Determine its harmonic conjugate and find the corresponding analytic function.
State and prove Cauchy Integral formula. Hence using it evaluate the integral ∮_c z³/(2z−i) dz, where c: |z| = 1, counter clockwise.

State Cauchy's Residue Theorem. Using residue theorem, evaluate:
(i) ∮_c 1/(z²+4) dz, where C: |z − i| = 2
(ii) ∮_c (4−3z)/(z²−z) dz, where C: |z| = 2

Find the Laurent's series expansion of f(z) = (z²−1)/[(z+2)(z+3)] in the following regions:
(i) |z| < 2   (ii) 2 < |z| < 3   (iii) |z| > 3
State and prove initial value theorem and find the inverse z-transform of F(z) = (z²−3z)/[(z−5)(z+2)].
Solve the difference equation y_{n+2} − 4y_{n+1} + 4y_n = 2ⁿ, y₀ = 0, y₁ = 1 by using Z-transform.
Define Fourier integral. By choosing a suitable function, show that ∫₀^∞ (cosxw + w sinxw)/(1+w²) dw equals: 0 if x < 0, π/2 if x = 0, πe^{−x} if x > 0.
questions.eq: \int_0^\infty \frac{\cos xw + w\sin xw}{1+w^2}\,dw = \begin{cases} 0 & x<0 \\ \pi/2 & x=0 \\ \pi e^{-x} & x>0 \end{cases}

questions.question: Find the Fourier sine transform of f(x) = e^{−x} for x > 0 and then show that ∫₀^∞ x sin(mx)/(1+x²) dx = (π/2)e^{−m}, x > 0.
Define partial differential equations. By separating the variables, solve: (i) u_{xy} − u = 0   (ii) xu_{xy} + 2yu = 0

A tightly stretched string of length L is drawn a side at its midpoint a distance of L/2 perpendicular to the equilibrium position so that its initial position is given by f(x) = x if 0 < x < L/2, (L−x) if L/2 < x < π. It is initially at rest and is suddenly released. Find u(x, t).
questions.eq: f(x) = \begin{cases} x & 0 < x < L/2 \\ L-x & L/2 < x < \pi \end{cases}

questions.question: Derive one dimensional heat equation ∂u/∂t = c² ∂²u/∂x² with solutions.
Starting from Cartesian form of Laplace equation derive the Laplace equation in polar form.

options.question: Find the linear fractional transformation that maps z₁ = 0, z₂ = −1, z₃ = i onto the points w₁ = i, w₂ = 0, w₃ = ∞.
Find Z-transform of sin(nπ/2) and cos(nπ/2).
Show that the Fourier cosine transforms satisfy linearity property.
Write equation of an ellipsoid. Sketch it with centre and axis of symmetry.

questions.question: Define harmonic function. Show that the function u = 3x²y + x² − y³ − y² is a harmonic function. Find the analytic function for which the given function is a real part.
State and prove Cauchy integral formula. Using it, evaluate the integral ∮_c (2z²+3)/(z−2)² dz where c: |z−1| = 2 in counter-clockwise direction.
Expand f(z) = 1/[(z+1)(z+3)] in Laurent series valid for (i) 1 < |z| < 3  (ii) |z| > 3  (iii) |z| < 1  (iv) 0 < |z+1| < 2
Let the rectangular region R in the z-plane be bounded by lines x = 0, y = 0, x = 2, y = 3. Find the region R' of the w-plane into which R is mapped under the transformation ω = √2 z e^{iπ/4}.
Find the Z transform of rⁿ cos nθ.
Find the inverse Z-transform of the function (2z²−5z)/[(z−2)(z−3)].
Solve the differential equation y_{k+2} + 2y_{k+1} + y_k = k where y₀ = 0, y₁ = 0 using Z-transform.
Show that ∫₀^∞ (1−cos πw)/w · sin πx dw = π/2 if 0 ≤ x ≤ π, 0 if x > π.
Find the Fourier cosine transform of f(x) = e^{−x} (x > 0) and hence by using Parseval's identity, show that ∫₀^∞ dx/(1+x²)² = π/4.
Define partial differential equation. Solve u_{xx} − u_{xy} = 0 by separating the variables.
Find u(x, t) from one dimensional wave equation ∂²u/∂t² = c² ∂²u/∂x², with boundary condition u(0,t) = 0 = u(L,t), initial deflection f(x) and initial velocity ∂u/∂t|_{t=0} = g(x).
questions.eq: \frac{\partial^2 u}{\partial t^2} = c^2 \frac{\partial^2 u}{\partial x^2},\quad u(0,t)=0=u(L,t),\quad \left.\frac{\partial u}{\partial t}\right|_{t=0}=g(x)

questions.question: Find the temperature in a laterally insulated bar of length L whose ends are kept at a zero temperature, assuming that the initial temperature is f(x) = x if 0 < x < L/2, (L−x) if L/2 < x < L.
questions.eq: f(x) = \begin{cases} x & 0 < x < L/2 \\ L-x & L/2 < x < L \end{cases}

questions.question: Express P²u = ∂²u/∂x² + ∂²u/∂y² in polar coordinates.

options.question: Check analyticity of f(z) = z².
Find tangent vector on the curve r⃗ = 2cos t î + 2sin t j̄ + t k̄, at P(2, 0, 0).
Find z-transform of z(aⁿ).
Find Fourier sine transform of e^{−|x|}.

questions.question: Define harmonic function. Show that v = 2xy − y/(x²+y²) is a harmonic function. Find harmonic conjugate of v.
Evaluate ∮_c cos(πz²)/(z²−3z+2) dz where C: |z| = 3.
Find the image of triangular region of the Z-plane bounded by the lines x = 0, y = 0, x + y = 1 under the transformation w = z e^{iπ/4} and show the sketch in the diagram.

State Cauchy Residue Theorem. By applying Cauchy Residue Theorem, evaluate ∮_c (4−3z)/[z(z−1)(z−2)] dz where C: |z| = 3/2.

OR

Find Laurent series of the function f(z) = 1/[z²(1−z)] in the region (i) 0 < |z| < 1  (ii) 1 < |z| < 4.
State and prove first shifting theorem of Z-transform. Using it evaluate Z[e^{−iat}] and hence find Z(sin at).
Use z-transform to solve the difference equation y_{n+2} − 2y_{n+1} + y_n = 2ⁿ, where y₀ = 0 and y₁ = 1.
Find the Fourier integral of the function f(x) = π/2 if 0 ≤ x ≤ 1, π/4 if x = 1, 0 if x > 1.
Find the Fourier cosine transform of f(x) = e^{−x} (x > 0) and hence by using Parseval's identity, show that ∫₀^∞ dx/(1+x²)² = π/4.
Define partial differential equation. Solve u_{xx} − u_{xy} = 0 by separating the variables.
Find u(x, t) from one dimensional wave equation ∂²u/∂t² = c² ∂²u/∂x², with boundary condition u(0,t) = 0 = u(L,t), initial deflection f(x) and initial velocity ∂u/∂t|_{t=0} = g(x).
Find the temperature in a laterally insulated bar of length L whose ends are kept at zero temperature, assuming that the initial temperature is f(x) = x if 0 < x < L/2, (L−x) if L/2 < x < L.
questions.eq: f(x) = \begin{cases} x & 0 < x < L/2 \\ L-x & L/2 < x < L \end{cases}

questions.question: Find the Fourier transform of the function f(x) = e^{−x²/2}.

options.question: Check analyticity of f(z) = z².
Find tangent vector on the curve r⃗ = cos t î + 2sin t j̄ + t k̄, at P(1/2, √3, 0).
Find z-transform of z(aⁿ).

questions.question: Define harmonic function. Check u = x³ − 3xy² is harmonic or not? If yes, find corresponding harmonic conjugate v of u.
State Cauchy Integral formula for derivative. Evaluate ∮_c z⁶/(2z−1)⁶ dz, where c is the unit circle |z| = 1, counterclockwise.

Solve the difference equation by using Z-transform: y_{n+2} − 4y_{n+1} + 4y_n = 2ⁿ with y₀ = 0, y₁ = 1.
Show that ∫₀^∞ sin πw sin xw/(1−w²) dw = π/2 sin x if 0 ≤ x ≤ π, 0 if x > π.
Find Fourier sine transform of f(x) = e^{−x} for x > 0. Then prove that ∫₀^∞ x sin mx/(1+x²) dx = (π/2) e^{−m} for m > 0.
Derive one dimensional heat equation ∂u/∂t = c² ∂²u/∂x² with necessary assumptions.
Find u(x, t) from one dimensional wave equation ∂²u/∂t² = c² ∂²u/∂x², with boundary condition u(0,t) = 0 = u(L,t), initial deflection f(x) and initial velocity ∂u/∂t|_{t=0} = g(x).

A homogeneous rod of conducting material of length 100 cm has its ends kept at zero temperature and the temperature initially is f(x) = x for 0 ≤ x ≤ 50, (100−x) for 50 ≤ x ≤ 100. Find the temperature distribution on the rod at any time.

Express the Laplacian ∇²u = ∂²u/∂x² + ∂²u/∂y² in polar co-ordinates.

OR

Find the unit tangent vector to the curve r⃗(t) = 2cos t î + sin t j̄ at (√2, √2, 0).

questions.question: Define harmonic function. Show that the function u = 3x²y + x² − y³ − y² is a harmonic function. Find the analytic function for which the given function is a real part.
Evaluate ∮_c cos(πz²)/(z²−3z+2) dz where C: |z| = 3.
Let the rectangular region R in the z-plane be bounded by lines x = 0, y = 0, x = 2, y = 3. Find the region R' of the w-plane into which R is mapped under the transformation W = √2 e^{iπ/4} z.

Find the Taylor's and Laurent's series of the function f(z) = (z²−1)/[(z+2)(z+3)].

OR

State Cauchy Residue Theorem. By applying Cauchy Residue Theorem, evaluate ∮_c (4−3z)/[z(z−1)(z−2)] dz where C: |z| = 3/2.
State and prove first shifting theorem on Z-transform. Find the Z-transform of e^{−at}.
Solve the differential equation y_{k+2} + 2y_{k+1} + y_k = k where y₀ = 0, y₁ = 0 using Z-transform.
Show that ∫₀^∞ sin πw sin xw/(1−w²) dw = π/2 sin x if 0 ≤ x ≤ π, 0 if x > π.
Find Fourier cosine transform of f(x) = e^{−x} (x > 0) and hence by using Parseval's identity, show that ∫₀^∞ dx/(1+x²)² = π/4.
Define partial differential equation with suitable example. By separating the variables solve u_{xx} + u_{yy} = 0.
Find u(x, t) from one dimensional wave equation ∂²u/∂t² = c² ∂²u/∂x², with boundary condition u(0,t) = 0 = u(L,t), initial deflection f(x) and initial velocity ∂u/∂t|_{t=0} = g(x).
Find the temperature in a laterally insulated bar of length L whose ends are kept at a zero temperature, assuming that the initial temperature is f(x) = x if 0 < x < L/2, (L−x) if L/2 < x < L.
Express the Laplacian ∇²u = ∂²u/∂x² + ∂²u/∂y² in polar co-ordinates.

options.question: If u = y³ − 3x²y show that u is harmonic.
Find z-transform of z(aⁿ).

Solve the partial differential equation u_{yy} = u.

questions.question: Define analytic function. Show that the function u(x,y) = 3x²y + x² − y³ − y² is a harmonic function. Find a function v(x,y) such that u + iv is an analytic function.

Derive and find the solution of one dimensional wave equation.

Solve xu_{xy} + 2yu = 0 using separating variables.
Find u(x,t) of the string of length l = π when c² = 1, the initial velocity is zero and the initial deflection is 0.1(π−x).
